author: Grub Basket
description: Quality-of-life tools for Bases and properties: find & replace property values across the vault, stop number properties from changing on arrow keys, and a searchable index of every property. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Human
manifest.description: Quality-of-life tools for Bases and properties: find & replace property values across the vault, stop number properties from changing on arrow keys or scroll wheel, and a searchable index of every property.
manifest.name: Bases Toolbox

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 0.1.18

Extends the 0.1.17 base-detection fix to the remaining features, so nothing
depends on the base being the *focused* leaf.

- **Export active base results as CSV** now works when a base is open but not
  focused (it previously errored with "Focus a base first").
- **Allowed-values picker on Bases cells** resolves the right `.base` from the
  cell you clicked, instead of the active file.
- **Bulk edit / Compute rollup** now prefer the on-screen base when several are
  open, rather than picking one blindly.

Under the hood these all share one detection helper now (`base-detect.ts`),
which prefers the focused base, then the visible base, then the sole open base —
so behavior is consistent across every command. No change when the base already
has focus.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 0.1.17

Bug fix: two features didn't detect an open base unless it was the *focused*
leaf.

- **"New note with properties" now borrows the base's columns reliably.** When a
  base was open but focus was elsewhere (a cell editor, the command palette, a
  side pane), the modal fell back to blank rows instead of the base's columns.
  It now finds the open base the same way Bulk Edit does, so the new note slots
  into the base as intended.
- **"Zoom into cell" no longer says "no base is open"** when a base is open but
  not the active leaf. It now resolves the `.base` from the leaf that actually
  contains the cell you clicked, instead of the (possibly different) active file.

Both relied on `activeLeaf` / `getActiveFile()` pointing at the base, which
isn't guaranteed; they now use `getLeavesOfType("bases")` as the reliable
signal. Verified live with a base open behind another focused view.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 0.1.16

Store-review compliance plus a few table/property polish items.

- **Store-review lint fix.** The one `eslint-disable` directive in the code now
  carries its rationale inline (via the `--` separator), which the Obsidian
  community-store review requires. No behavior change.
- **Filter quick-toggle keeps a stable order.** Toggling a filter on/off no
  longer re-ranks the list (enabled-over-disabled), so rows don't jump under the
  cursor and you can't accidentally click the wrong one.
- **Property index — rename value box spans the modal**, and value inputs now
  support `[[` internal-link autocomplete when renaming a value.
- **Multiline list cells — one-time tip.** Enabling the setting now shows a
  brief pointer to Bases' own row-height option, since stacking the list values
  doesn't grow the (virtualized) row height on its own.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 0.1.15

Hardening from a full-codebase audit — no user-facing feature changes.

- **Reserved property names are rejected** across all property-writing tools
  (bulk edit, the properties modal, rollup, metadata stamp): a name like
  `__proto__` used to silently succeed while writing nothing. It's now refused
  with a clear notice.
- **Base export honors `not(...)` filters.** A base filtering *out* a folder
  (`not(file.inFolder("Archive"))`) exported *only* that folder; it now scopes
  correctly (whole-vault superset, flagged as approximate).
- **Merge revert survives a deleted folder.** If a merged note's folder was
  deleted afterward, reverting the merge now recreates the folder and both
  notes instead of aborting halfway.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 0.1.14

## Edit / create note properties (roomy form)
- **New note with properties** and **Edit properties of the active note** open a
  proper form for a note's frontmatter — a comfortable alternative to Obsidian's
  cramped inline / Bases-popup editor. Type-aware value widgets
  (text / number / checkbox / date / list / tags / aliases), add/remove rows,
  and a **File name** field that renames the note (the file-name editing that
  zoom-into-cell can't do).
- Reachable from two commands, a note's right-click menu, and the launcher.
- **Internal links**: type `[[` in a text or list value to autocomplete a note
  and insert a wikilink — it keeps working across multiple links in a list.
  A 🔗 button also inserts one. (Tags and aliases don't offer links.)
- **From a base**: launched while a base is focused, it borrows that view's
  editable columns and the base's folder, so the note slots straight in — like
  adding a row. Otherwise it opens with a few blank rows (count configurable in
  settings).

## Launcher favorites
- Star any feature to pin it to a **★ Favorites** section at the top; **drag to
  re-arrange**. "New note with properties" is pinned by default.

## Conditional formatting
- The **"is duplicated"** rule now re-evaluates in **real time** — it no longer
  needed a reload to catch a value becoming (or ceasing to be) a duplicate.

## Companions
- `.base` files are excluded from companioning by **default** (a one-time
  change you can undo by removing "base" from the exclude list). `.edtz` stays
  hard-excluded.

## Smaller
- CSV export/import folder & base pickers are wider, so the hint/path is
  readable.
- CSV export "From a base" writes a readable `.md` summary and lists what a flat
  CSV can't hold; the folder picker starts blank; column checklist per export.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 0.1.13

## Export — a lot more capable
- **From a base**: load a base, pick **which view** to export (bases can have
  several), and see that view's filters, columns, and the base's filters before
  exporting. Multi-folder bases are handled.
- **From a folder**: check off **subfolders to ignore**, and see the
  **non-markdown files** a CSV skips — one button **companions** them so they're
  included on the next load, regardless of your companion settings.
- **Column checklist** (both modes) — all on by default; pick exactly what to
  export.
- **Readable base summary (.md)** — a plain-language note of the base's filters,
  formulas, sort, and grouping, so you can rebuild in a spreadsheet what a flat
  CSV can't hold. The exporter now spells out these limitations inline.
- Folder box starts blank (blank = whole vault).

## Import
- **Drag and drop** a CSV/TSV onto the importer, or click the same zone to
  choose a file. Clearer supported-format text; binary spreadsheets are rejected
  with a helpful note. The **target folder autocompletes**.

## Reference
- **"Bases built-in properties"** section in the property index — click any
  `file.*` identifier (created time, tags, folder, …) to copy it, for when the
  Bases property menu "forgets" it.
- **Base embeds reference**: Settings → *Generate reference note* writes a
  packaged (offline) note documenting the embed display flags with examples.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 0.1.12

## CSV import & export — separate, first-class panels
- **Import** and **Export** are each their own feature now, openable in the
  **sidebar, a tab, or a new window** (or a dialog).
- **Export → From a base**: pick any `.base` file (no need to open it). It reads
  the folders the base references and its column order, resolves the built-in
  `file.*` columns (name, path, folder, ext, size, created, modified, tags) plus
  your frontmatter properties, and exports the notes it covers. Multi-folder
  bases are handled; a base with filters beyond folder scope is flagged as a
  best-effort superset.
- **Export → From a folder**: pick any folder — every note's frontmatter becomes
  a CSV row with a column per key found. Preview, then **Copy for Excel** or
  write a `.csv`.
- **Export active base results** (command): still available for a base's exact
  live filtered results with formula columns; errors on a non-base view.
- **Import** now autocompletes the target folder and rejects spreadsheet files
  (`.xlsx`/`.numbers`/…) with a "convert to CSV first" note instead of reading
  binary as garbage.

## Bases built-in properties
- The **property index** has a new collapsible **"Bases built-in properties"**
  section: click any `file.*` identifier (created time, tags, folder, …) to copy
  it, then paste it into a base when its property menu "forgets" it. They're
  identifiers, not formulas.
- The settings **Reference** and the README document the same list, with
  `.base` YAML examples and when to use a formula instead.

## Reverts are clearer & safer
- Each history entry shows a **red data-loss warning on its own line** when
  reverting it could lose data — always for merges, and for other operations
  only when "force" is on.
- After a revert, the entry lists **which files were skipped and why**
  (edited since / renamed / missing), each clickable.
- Long paths and change values now wrap instead of colliding.

## Tips (README)
- Fork a note with the Commander plugin (Create new + Rename).
- Use Obsidian's built-in formula editor or Formula Forge for formulas.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 0.1.11

## Safer, clearer reverts (History)
- **Per-entry data-loss warning.** Each history entry now shows a red warning
  after its name *only when reverting it could lose data*:
  - **Merges** — always (reverting restores the whole note, overwriting any
    edits you made after the merge).
  - **Everything else** — only when "Also revert notes I've edited since the
    change" (force) is turned on. With it off, those notes are safely skipped,
    so no warning.
  The warnings appear and disappear live as you toggle that option.
- **Skipped files are shown after a revert.** Instead of a fleeting notice, the
  entry now lists every file that was *not* reverted — each clickable to open —
  tagged with why: edited since (left untouched to protect your newer edit),
  property renamed/removed, file moved/deleted, or (for merges) a note now
  occupies that path. When drift caused the skip, a note explains how to force
  it if you really want to.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 0.1.10

## Find duplicate notes — big upgrade
- **Merge preview**: pick which note to keep and see exactly what will happen —
  every resulting property tagged by origin (kept, added, merged list, or a
  conflict the kept note wins), which bodies get combined, and what moves to
  trash. No more diffing in your head.
- **Bodies merge in creation-date order** (kept note included), so the combined
  note reads chronologically without you ordering anything. A warning appears
  when creation-date order differs from last-edited order.
- **Clickable note links** — open any candidate in a new tab.
- **Runs as its own tab** (command + launcher Sidebar/Tab/Window buttons), not
  just a dialog; the dialog gains an "Open in a tab" shortcut.
- **No note is pre-selected** as the one to keep — you choose deliberately, and
  the merge button stays disabled until you do.
- **Ignore** groups you don't want to act on: an Ignore button moves a group to
  an "Ignored" tab. Ignores persist and auto-re-flag if the group's membership
  changes. Two tabs — "To review" and "Ignored" — at the top.
- The tab content is centered in a readable column.

## Merges are now fully revertible
- Both the duplicate-finder merge and "Merge current note into another" are
  recorded to **History** as whole-note snapshots. Reverting restores the kept
  note, **recreates the trashed sources**, and undoes backlink rewrites in
  other notes — the entire merge, in one click.
- The merge/revert confirmations now show the result **in the button** (it turns
  green on success) and use clearer wording.

## Launcher
- Each panel can now open in a **new window** (Sidebar / Tab / Window).
- New **wrench** icon (the old one looked like Canvas/Workspaces).

## Clearer History
- "Force-revert drifted files" is relabeled and explained: reverting normally
  skips notes you edited again after the change (so it never overwrites newer
  work); this option reverts them too. Merge reverts always restore the whole
  note and ignore this setting.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 0.1.9

## Find duplicate notes
- Note entries are now **clickable links** — click one to open that note in a
  new tab instead of squinting at a plain path.
- The finder can run as **its own tab** (not just a dialog): a new command
  **"Find duplicate notes (in a tab)"**, plus **Sidebar / Tab** buttons in the
  launcher. In a tab, opened notes sit beside the finder instead of behind it.
  The dialog gains an **"Open in a tab"** shortcut.
- **No note is pre-selected** as the one to keep — you pick it deliberately,
  and the merge button stays disabled until you do (changing your pick also
  re-arms the confirm step).
- Tab content is centered in a readable column instead of stretching across
  the whole window.

## Docs
- The README now has a **Permissions & data** section: no network access or
  telemetry, vault file listing is local-only, and clipboard use is write-only.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 0.1.8

## Launcher
- A friendly **Bases Toolbox launcher** in the left sidebar (new ribbon icon +
  "Open Bases Toolbox launcher") lists every feature as a card with a one-line
  description and a button to open it — grouped into **Panels & windows** (each
  with a Sidebar and a Tab button), **Dialogs & actions**, and **Settings**.
  Great for people who don't want to hunt the command palette.

## Conditional formatting
- New **"is duplicated (in this base)"** rule — colors any row or cell whose
  value appears more than once among the base's currently-shown rows (spot
  duplicate account names, IDs, etc. at a glance).

## Find & replace
- Cleaner layout: each control on its own row (Property, Find, then Replace
  with + the text box).
- **"Empty replacement clears the value"** now shows as a red warning with an
  icon when the box is empty (it's a destructive clear), and tempers to a plain
  note once you type a value.
- The "N files will change" summary is bigger and sits right above the list.
- The command is now **"Find and replace property values"** so typing "find and
  replace" finds it in the palette.

## Property format doctor
- Each property group heading shows its **type icon** (matching the property
  index).

## Fixes
- **Rollup / bulk edit** no longer say "open a base" when a base *is* open —
  they now find the open base even when it isn't the focused view (e.g. launched
  from the launcher or a sidebar).

## Settings
- The in-app **reference** now explains every feature in plain language with
  examples (rollup, forks, companions, allowed values, and more), so
  non-technical users can learn what each tool does without leaving Obsidian.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 0.1.7

## Views — move between main tab and sidebar
- Every plugin view (Property Index, Find & Replace, History, Format Doctor,
  Conditional Formatting) gains a **"Move to sidebar"** action in its tab header
  — dock a main-tab view into the right sidebar. The main-tab-primary views also
  get the reverse **"Open in a main tab"**, so you can move them either way.

## Property format doctor
- **Ignore** a mismatch you're fine leaving as-is. It's hidden from the list but
  keyed to the value — so if that value ever **changes ("breaks"), it re-flags
  automatically**. No permanent ignore that silently goes stale.
- New **"To fix" and "Ignored" tabs** at the top. The ignored values live on
  their own tab, each with an **Un-ignore** button, plus **Clear ignore list**.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 0.1.6

Improvements to the pinned allowed-values audit and the pin indicator.

## Pinned allowed-values audit
- The audit now opens with a **"Pinned properties"** overview listing *every*
  property that has a pin — compliant ones ("all values OK") and violating ones
  ("N outside the list", flagged) — each with an **Edit pinned values** button,
  so you can see and manage all your pins in one place instead of hunting the
  property index for pin icons.
- Each value outside its allowed list has an **"Add to allowed"** button next to
  "Find & replace" — adopt the value into the property's allowed list instead of
  replacing it, and it drops off the violations right away.
- Property group headers are flush-left with a property icon (no more odd
  indentation).

## Pin indicator
- The pin icon has three clear states: **muted** (not pinned), **accent**
  (pinned, all values within the list), and **red** (pinned, some values
  outside). The accent now applies reliably.
- The violation toast and the red pin icon update **immediately** after a pin
  change (add to allowed, save, or clear) — before, they only refreshed on a
  vault change and could linger stale. While the audit modal is open the toast
  stays hidden, since you're already resolving violations there.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 0.1.5

A big batch of bug fixes and UI polish from a review pass.

## Bug fixes

- **Deleting a live fork target no longer "un-deletes" itself.** Deleting a
  property an active fork writes to (e.g. `amount-fork-as-links`) used to be
  recomputed from the source within a beat, so the deletion — and reverting it —
  never stuck. Deleting such a property now opens a fork-aware dialog: **Pause
  sync & delete** (keeps the rule, restorable) or **Remove rule & delete** (moves
  the rule to the restorable removed-list). Covers property/value/file scope.
- **The history tab refreshes in realtime.** A delete or find & replace in another
  tab now updates the history list (and its drift badges) without reopening it.
- **Notes open in the view's own window.** Opening a file from a popped-out plugin
  view now lands the tab in that popout window instead of jumping to the main one
  (Format Doctor links, Property Index open buttons). The sidebar-docked index
  still opens notes in the main editor area.

## Views: refresh-on-refocus + auto-return-focus

- All five views (Property Index, Find & Replace, History, Format Doctor,
  Conditional Formatting) re-render when their tab regains focus, fixing stale or
  blank deferred-leaf state.
- Opening a note from a view and then closing it returns focus to the view.

## Property Index

- Fuzzy filter: "fruit basket" matches a `fruit-basket` property (whitespace
  tokens, all must match, `-`/`_`/space treated the same).
- A clear (X) button on the filter, and an expand chevron on each property header.
- Property and value rows now use the same icons in the same order (open ·
  replace · rename · copy · delete); open tooltips read "Open N file(s) in new
  tabs with this property/value name".
- Expanded file rows swap their redundant trash for a "⋯" file menu (open in
  tab/right/below); the value keeps its trash. The value right-click menu drops
  its long inline file list and keeps Open-all · Copy · Rename · Delete.

## Format Doctor

- Each row shows a live **output preview** (current → result) so you can see what
  a value becomes before applying; empty inputs read "(skipped)", unparseable
  ones "(invalid)".
- **Select all / Deselect all** buttons (select-all skips still-blank
  needs-a-human rows).

## Allowed values

- The pin icon turns a clear warning **red** when a pinned property has values
  outside its allowed list, and a persistent notice offers a one-click **Open
  audit**.
- The audit command is renamed "Audit pinned allowed values" for discoverability.

## Find & Replace history

- Renamed to **Property & Value History** (command: "Property, value & frontmatter
  history"); the "Force-revert drifted files" toggle moved to its own line.

## Also

- Post-deploy reload notices carry a **Reload now** button (dev tooling).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 0.1.3

Property fork management, property-index file access, and conditional-formatting
per-base scoping.

## Property forks
- Live sync is now **on by default** when you fork a property, so forks appear
  as manageable rows in settings.
- Each fork can be **paused** (keeps the definition, stops recomputing) instead
  of only deleted; **deleting** moves it to a restorable "recently removed" list.
- Forks are **editable** — repoint the source/target property or change the
  transform without recreating the fork.
- A **"Fork builder…"** button in settings opens the convert/fork picker.
- If a fork's source or target property no longer exists (e.g. after renaming a
  property in the All Properties view), the fork row shows a **warning** so a
  broken fork is easy to spot and fix or pause.

## Property index
- Expand any value to **list the files that use it**, and open them — click,
  cmd/ctrl-click for a new tab, an open button, or a right-click menu
  (Open / new tab / to the right / below).
- Per-value and per-property counts now spell out what they mean ("N files
  have this value").
- A toolbar button **opens the property index in a main tab**.

## Conditional formatting
- Each rule can apply to **all bases or a chosen subset** via a per-rule sheet
  picker.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 0.1.2

New features for non-Markdown files and metadata durability, a settings/command
consistency pass, and a substantial conditional-formatting overhaul.

## Companion notes for non-Markdown files
- New command **Create companion notes for non-Markdown files**: Bases can't
  read frontmatter from images, PDFs, audio, etc., so this creates a small
  companion note per file (`<file>.<ext>.md`) carrying the file's metadata as
  frontmatter — `file-name`, `file-ext`, `file-size`, `file-created`,
  `file-modified`, `file-path` — plus a `companion-of` link and an embed of the
  file. Companions live adjacent to their files by default, or in a designated
  folder that mirrors the source structure.
- Scan-first preview shows how many companions will be created vs refreshed.
  Re-running refreshes the `file-*` properties and never touches properties you
  added. Files derived from notes (e.g. version files other plugins create) are
  skipped so companioning can't cascade.
- Settings section with a default destination, an extension filter, and an
  opt-in **auto mode** that companions newly added files (armed after startup so
  the launch-time file-scan isn't mistaken for a bulk drop).

## Stamp file metadata into note properties
- New command **Stamp file metadata into note properties**: filesystem
  created/modified times get destroyed by syncs, copies, and migrations, while
  frontmatter travels with the note. This snapshots each Markdown note's current
  times into properties you name (default `created` / `modified`), scoped to a
  folder or the whole vault. Set-if-missing by default; overwrite is opt-in.
  Logged to history and revertible.

## Conditional formatting overhaul
- **Row or cell scope per rule**: color the whole row (default) or just the
  matching property's cell.
- **Real-time updates**: rule changes now apply to open bases without an app
  reload — including bases in popout windows. (Previously a new rule often
  didn't show until reload.)
- **Rules are fully editable inline** — property, operator, and value can now be
  changed on an existing rule, not just its color/enabled state.
- **Reorder rules** with up/down controls (rules apply top-to-bottom; first
  match wins per row and per cell).
- **Color swatch preview** on each rule; **capitalized color names**
  (Red / Orange / … / Custom…).
- **Custom color picker** no longer renders clipped; **dropdowns size to their
  selected text** instead of a fixed-wide box; the **"Add rule" label** no
  longer truncates.

## Settings & commands consistency
- **Pinned allowed values** now have a settings section listing every pinned
  property with a remove control (previously createable only from the property
  index, with no way to review or remove them in settings).
- **Toggle commands** for the number guard, digits-only typing, and multiline
  list cells — so they can be bound to hotkeys.
- New commands **Open Bases Toolbox settings** and **Open conditional formatting
  rules** (the latter deep-links to the section, scrolls it into view, and
  flashes it) — conditional formatting previously had no command entry point.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 0.1.1 — store-review fixes

Addresses the community-store review findings. No feature changes.

- Replaces all direct inline-style assignments with Obsidian's `setCssStyles`
  (conditional-formatting row tints, embed height flags, the allowed-values
  picker position, and show/hide of conditional settings rows) — the review's
  one blocking error.
- Popout-window compatibility: DOM listeners, queries, and body classes use
  `activeDocument`; timers use `window.setTimeout`; element type checks use
  Obsidian's cross-window `instanceOf`.
- Note merging now uses `FileManager.trashFile()` so deletions respect the
  user's configured trash preference (system trash / vault trash / permanent).
- Replaces deprecated `Workspace.activeLeaf` with `getActiveViewOfType` in
  bulk edit and CSV export.
- Number guard: allows the comma decimal separator explicitly and drops an
  unnecessary regex escape.
- Async click handlers wrapped to satisfy void-return expectations; removes
  unused code (`ALL_VALUES` sentinel, an unused type import); fixes a
  redundant `unknown | null` union; removes the one `!important` from the
  stylesheet in favor of a more specific selector.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 0.1.0 — first release

Quality-of-life tools for Bases and properties. Everything below landed in
this initial release, in roughly the order it was built.

## Find & replace, history, and reverts
- Find & replace property values across the vault: pick a property, replace
  one specific value everywhere or override all values at once; list
  properties replace the matched item in place (initially with a
  single-level undo, later superseded by the full history below).
- Operation history: every run is logged with per-file before/after values;
  entries can be reverted individually. A user-configurable history cap
  (default: no cap) and a clear-history action live in the settings.
- Find & replace becomes a main-area tab: matched files appear as a
  checklist with `current → new` preview per file, partial apply, and live
  validation of the replacement value ("existing value — matches will merge"
  vs "new value").
- History becomes a main-area tab with selective per-file revert, a
  force-revert toggle for files edited since the operation, "edited since"
  drift badges, `old → new` rows, and a provenance chip showing which
  feature produced each entry. An entry only counts as reverted once nothing
  from it remains in effect, so partial and forced retries always work.
- A memoized property cache (invalidated by metadata events) backs the
  index, find & replace, and audits, so repeated opens cost nothing.

## Number input guard
- Number properties — in the frontmatter panel and inside Bases views — no
  longer change value on ArrowUp/ArrowDown or the mouse scroll wheel.
- Optional digits-only typing swallows stray characters like `e`; `.`, `,`
  (locale decimal) and `-` still type fine. Both toggles default on.

## Bulk editing
- Bulk edit the properties of a base's current results — whatever the open
  view shows after filters and search. Five modes: set, set only if missing,
  append items, remove items, delete the property entirely. All revertible;
  reverting a bulk-create deletes the property again, reverting a delete
  restores it.

## Rollups
- Compute count / sum / average / min / max over the notes linking to (or
  linked from) each result of the open base, written into a real frontmatter
  property Bases can display and sort. One-shot, re-run to refresh,
  revertible. Only genuine numbers aggregate — `null`, empty strings, and
  booleans are skipped.

## Conditional formatting
- Color Bases rows by rules (first match wins): property, operator
  (equals / not-equals / contains / comparisons / empty), value, and a color
  from the theme palette — or any custom color via a picker, applied at the
  same subtle tint strength. `not-equals` on list values requires every item
  to differ.

## CSV import & export
- Import CSV/TSV as one note per row: per-column property names (Title Case
  by default), types (text / number / date / boolean / list / link), include
  toggles with select-all, filename column, sample values under each header,
  live preview with row navigation, an optional note-body template with
  `{{Column}}` placeholders, an omit-empty option, a collision policy
  (suffix / skip / overwrite) probed against both the batch and existing
  vault files, and an optional auto-created `.base` over the imported
  folder. Dates normalize from US, European, written-month, and Excel-serial
  formats, with a warning counting ambiguous `M/D` vs `D/M` dates. Quoted
  fields may contain newlines. (Ported from the companion
  CSV-to-Obsidian-Properties-for-Bases web tool, then re-synced with its v2
  update.)
- Export the open base's results as clean CSV — wikilinks unwrapped, lists
  joined, spreadsheet formula injection neutralized — written next to the
  `.base` file and copied to the clipboard.

## Merge & duplicates
- Merge one note into another, frontmatter-aware: missing properties copy
  over, list properties union, scalar conflicts get a per-property picker.
  The source's body is appended, every link to the source — body links and
  frontmatter links — is re-pointed at the target with aliases preserved,
  and the source moves to the vault trash (recoverable).
- Find duplicate notes by similar file names (case, punctuation, and
  copy/number suffixes ignored), by equal values of a chosen property,
  and/or by identical bodies (verified byte-for-byte within hash groups).
  Pick the keeper per group; merging requires a confirming second click.

## Property index & allowed values
- Property index sidebar: every frontmatter property in the vault with type,
  file count, and per-value usage counts; search-filterable; per-property
  shortcuts into find & replace, a copy-name button, a button that opens the
  core All Properties view pre-filtered, and a pin button.
- Pinned allowed values: pin a property's permitted values; editing that
  property in the frontmatter panel or a Bases cell shows a picker; an audit
  command lists every out-of-list value with one-click find & replace jumps.

## Format doctor
- Property format doctor: finds every value that doesn't match its
  property's assigned type (the mismatches Obsidian flags but "fixes"
  destructively), explains what each type expects with an example, and
  pre-fills a safe suggested fix when one exists — dates normalized, `yes` →
  `true`, scalars → one-item lists, a date in a datetime slot gains
  `T00:00`. Inputs are validated before writing; invalid ones are marked and
  the file left untouched; empty means skip — a value is never erased.
  Applied fixes are revertible.

## Property fork / convert
- Convert or fork a property's format: normalize legacy dates to
  `YYYY-MM-DD`, unwrap `[[wikilinks]]` to plain text, or wrap plain text in
  wikilinks — in place, or forked into a second property so both formats
  coexist. Forks can be kept in live sync: editing the original recomputes
  the fork automatically. Active syncs are listed and removable in settings.

## Migration & display extras
- Inline-field migrator: converts Dataview-style `Key:: value` lines and
  `[key:: value]` spans into real frontmatter, with folder scoping, dry-run
  preview, typed values, optional body cleanup (off by default), and
  history-logged writes.
- Filter quick-toggle: disable individual `.base` filter conditions without
  deleting them; disabled filters are remembered and re-enable in place.
- Cell zoom editor: open a focused Bases cell or property value in a large
  editor modal; lists edit one item per line; nested objects are protected.
- Embedded-base display flags via embed alt text: `bases-no-toolbar`,
  `bases-no-header`, and `bt-height-N` for a fixed height.
- Multiline list cells: a toggle that stacks list-property pills vertically
  in Bases table cells (long lists scroll in-cell; pairs with the native
  row-height option).

## Reviews, hardening, and housekeeping
- Full code + security review before release: spreadsheet formula-injection
  escaping on export, exact-body verification in the duplicate finder,
  `__proto__`/`constructor` property names refused, a bounded inline-field
  scanner, plus seven correctness fixes — filter-toggle state desync, silent
  loss of duplicate filter conditions, rollups counting `null` as 0, Excel
  serial dates off by a day west of UTC, the comma decimal separator being
  blocked, bulk "set empty" creating null properties, and bracketed-field
  cleanup missing non-canonical spacing. Details in
  `docs/review-2026-07-02.md`.
- MIT license; credits with capability lists and links for related plugins
  (Mass Editor — developed independently, reviewed later for ideas —
  Better Properties, Metadata Menu, Multi Properties, Dataview to
  Properties, Bases Lock, Colored Bases Properties, Dualyze Notes, Merge
  Notes, and the companion CSV web tool).
- Docs throughout: README feature guide, community-gripes research and
  overlap analysis, forum outreach map, backlog, and internal fixes (NUL-byte
  dropdown sentinels, neutral doc voice, an unused import, TODO status
  corrections) — all part of this release's history.
